ops.body: T3 watcher parity rejection — diagnosis complete (parity-diag/codex, tree untouched). Root cause ranked: (1) HIGH — structural reference/serving mismatch: Tinker scores against full-precision Qwen/Qwen3-8B, local serving runs mlx-community/Qwen3-8B-4bit (quantized lm_head+embedding included); the gate compares first-token P(NO_STEER) on 20 seed-1729 rows across that precision gap. (2) MED — this candidate's LoRA deltas are ~13x smaller than v001's (dense-delta RMS 0.000567 vs 0.00746), so base quantization noise dominates its signal. (3) LOW-MED — bf16 softmax normalization in drafter_mlx contributes up to 0.0299 locally (measured), insufficient alone. (4) LOW — 4-bit base converted under mlx-lm 0.24.0, loaded under 0.31.3. RULED OUT: PEFT→MLX conversion loss (504/504 tensors, exact-zero elementwise error), adapter quantization, transpose bugs, render/tokenizer drift. Also established: v001 is NOT a valid control — different base (4B-Instruct), native-MLX trained, render v1, never ran this parity lane; this candidate may be the first ever through the check. Key observability hole: Tinker per-row probs are never persisted on rejection (write_probs only runs post-registration, watcher.py:342/375), so only the max (0.26032) is known. DECISION: Stage A (in flight) — re-score the exact 20 parity rows from the existing checkpoint sampler_weights/step1758 via compute_logprobs (cents), compare vs MLX under bf16 AND f32 normalization on both the current 4-bit base and one rebuilt under mlx-lm 0.31.3 → get the full gap distribution without retraining. Stage B (after data): production fix = f32 normalization + persist per-row parity diagnostics before rejection; then decide serve-8bit vs reference-against-served if broad drift remains. Tolerance raise explicitly rejected as a fix.
